intro: Vandenboschia fargesii, also known as Farges' spleenwort or Farges' polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to China, belonging to the family Aspleniaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which may help to alleviate fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vandenboschia fargesii include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in combination with other herbs in formulations such as capsules, tablets, or tonics.
